data.title: US Senior General Kane: Law Enforcement Will Be Conducted in Iranian Territorial Waters and International Waters
data.description: U.S. General Kane: The U.S. military is ready to resume operations. Enforcement will be carried out in Iranian territorial waters and international waters, with blockades applicable to all vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports. U.S. General Kane: The U.S. will track any vessels attempting to provide support to Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Defense Secretary Warns Iran of Potential Military Action
data.description:
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has announced that the United States is reinforcing its military capabilities with unprecedented strength. According to Foresight News, Hegseth stated that if Iran makes a strategic error and refuses to reach an agreement, the U.S. is prepared to resume military operations. He conveyed a clear message to Iran, emphasizing that the U.S. is closely monitoring their activities.

Hegseth highlighted that the U.S. is aware of Iran's military asset movements and noted that Iran is repairing launch devices damaged by bombings. He criticized Iran's claims of controlling the Strait of Hormuz, pointing out that Iran lacks a navy and that threatening commercial vessels is an act of piracy, not control.

Furthermore, Hegseth indicated that the U.S. is ready to target critical infrastructure, including power generation and the energy sector, if necessary.

data.title: BASED Token Surges with Significant Market Cap Increase
data.description: BASED token has experienced a notable increase, surpassing 0.14 USDT. According to Foresight News, its market capitalization has risen to $8.45 million, marking a 24-hour growth of 86.8%.

data.title: Galaxy Digital Urges SEC to Clarify Regulations on Tokenized Stocks
data.description: Galaxy Digital's research head, Alex Thorn, has highlighted the stance of traditional finance groups, which are publicly endorsing cryptocurrency while advocating for stringent application of securities laws by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) concerning tokenized stocks traded via decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms and automated market makers (AMMs). According to NS3.AI, Galaxy Digital has submitted a letter to the SEC, asserting that AMMs should not be classified as exchanges and that liquidity providers should not be considered dealers.

data.title: ECB's Kazaks: Companies May Adjust Prices More Rapidly Due to Recent Inflation Experience
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Martins Kazaks has indicated that businesses might begin adjusting their prices more swiftly than in the past, considering the recent experiences with inflation. According to Jin10, Kazaks highlighted that the inflationary pressures observed recently could lead to quicker price adjustments by companies, reflecting a shift in their pricing strategies. This observation comes amid ongoing discussions about inflation trends and their impact on the economy.

data.title: Following OpenAI's announcement of its first permanent office in London, Anthropic has also expanded its London operations.
data.description: Anthropic announced on Thursday that it is expanding its London operations with a new office space that can accommodate 800 people. This comes days after rival OpenAI announced plans to open its first permanent office in London. Anthropic stated that it already has over 200 employees working in London. Pip White, head of Anthropic's Europe, Middle East and North Africa region, said in the statement, "London is already one of our most important research and business hubs outside the US, and our expansion in the Knowledge Park provides space for our future growth." He added, "The UK brings together ambitious companies and institutions that understand the risks to AI security and has an excellent pool of AI talent – ​​we want to be the crossroads of it all." Earlier reports indicated that British officials attempted to recruit Anthropic after a dispute with the Pentagon over the use of its AI models. (Jinshi)

data.title: Paris Blockchain Week Enhances Security Amid Rising Crypto-Related Incidents in France
data.description: Paris Blockchain Week has increased security measures for its event this week, responding to a surge in crypto-related attacks in France, which have totaled 19 incidents in 2026. According to NS3.AI, the conference has implemented police-escorted buses to transport attendees to a dinner, aiming to ensure their safety. Jean-Didier Berger and Michael Amar reported that 466 members of the crypto industry have joined a priority emergency response platform, and authorities have arrested 230 individuals since January.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Expands London Operations Amid AI Talent Growth
data.description:
Anthropic announced on Thursday that it is expanding its operations in London, with a new office space capable of accommodating 800 employees. According to Jin10, this move comes just days after competitor OpenAI revealed plans to establish its first permanent office in London. Currently, over 200 Anthropic employees are based in the city.

Pip White, Anthropic's Head for Northern Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, stated in a release that London has become one of the company's most significant research and commercial hubs outside the United States. The expansion in the knowledge district provides room for future growth. White added that the UK is home to ambitious enterprises and institutions that understand the risks associated with AI safety and boasts an excellent pool of AI talent, which Anthropic aims to be at the center of.

Reports have previously indicated that UK officials attempted to attract Anthropic following a dispute between the company and the U.S. Department of Defense over the use of its AI models.

data.title: U.S. Military Expands Maritime Blockade on Iran
data.description: The U.S. military has announced an expansion of its maritime blockade on Iran, broadening the scope to include ammunition, weapons, and other smuggled goods. According to BlockBeats, vessels suspected of transporting prohibited items to Iran will be subject to the right of visit and search by belligerent nations, regardless of their location, and may face seizure. The list of banned items also encompasses oil, refined petroleum products, iron, steel, and aluminum.

data.title: IMF Advises Asian Central Banks on Energy Shock Response
data.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has recommended that Asian central banks exercise patience in responding to energy shocks. According to Jin10, the IMF emphasized the importance of maintaining anchored inflation expectations. However, it advised that if these expectations become unanchored, central banks should consider tightening their monetary policies. This guidance comes amid ongoing concerns about the impact of energy price fluctuations on regional economies.

data.title: The US military has expanded its maritime blockade of Iran to include weapons and other items within the blockade.
data.description: According to market sources, the US military has expanded its maritime blockade against Iran to include contraband such as munitions and weapons. Any vessel suspected of transporting contraband to Iran, regardless of its location, is subject to the belligerent nation's "right of access and search" and may be seized.

data.title: The Bless project team transferred 300 million BLESS tokens, with 200 million going to CEX and 100 million being sold on BSC.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, the Bless project team has transferred an additional 50 million BLESS tokens (worth $590,000) from the Solana chain to the BSC chain and continues to sell them on-chain. Currently, 56.76 million BLESS tokens have been sold on BSC, yielding 228,000 USDT and 416.4 BNB (worth $258,000). This morning, the project team transferred a total of 300 million BLESS tokens, with 200 million transferred to Bitget and the remaining 100 million currently being sold on the BSC chain.

data.title: Raoul Pal Highlights Key Metrics for Blockchain Valuation
data.description: Raoul Pal, a prominent figure in the financial sector, has identified two crucial metrics that will shape the future valuation of blockchain technology: the value transacted per user and the total number of users. According to NS3.AI, Pal emphasized that Metcalfe's Law serves as the most effective model for assessing blockchain valuation. He noted that the increasing use of stablecoins and tokenization is enhancing transaction values, while the growth of AI agents is contributing to an expanding user base.

data.title: AI TRENDS | EU Proposes Measures to Curb Google's Influence by Opening Search Data
data.description: On April 16, Jin10 reported that the European Union has proposed measures requiring Google to open its search data to competitors, including AI chatbot developers, in an effort to curb the influence of the U.S. tech giant. According to Jin10, the European Commission announced on Thursday that it has sent preliminary investigation results to Google's parent company, Alphabet, demanding compliance with the Digital Markets Act by the end of July. The proposed measures would allow third-party search engines access to data such as rankings and queries to "optimize services and compete with Google Search." If approved, this data could be made available to companies like OpenAI and Anthropic. Google's Senior Competition Legal Counsel, Claire Kelly, responded by stating that the company will "defend itself against overreach," arguing that the move "endangers privacy and security." The EU had already initiated a related review in January of this year. Additionally, Google faces potential penalties under the Digital Markets Act for favoring its own services and restricting app developers.

data.title: ECB Official Stresses Importance of Commitment to 2% Inflation Target
data.description: An official from the European Central Bank (ECB) emphasized the critical importance of maintaining a commitment to the 2% inflation target. According to Jin10, the official highlighted that clear communication regarding this target is essential for ensuring economic stability and guiding market expectations. The ECB's dedication to this inflation goal is seen as a cornerstone of its monetary policy strategy, aiming to foster sustainable economic growth and price stability across the Eurozone. The official's remarks underscore the ongoing efforts by the ECB to manage inflationary pressures and support the region's economic recovery.

data.title: U.S. Continues Efforts to Broker Ceasefire, Says Lebanese Presidential Office
data.description: The Lebanese presidential office has announced that the United States is actively working to facilitate a ceasefire. According to Odaily, this development highlights ongoing diplomatic efforts in the region. Further details on the progress of these efforts have not been disclosed.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Canadian Agency Forecasts Gold Prices to Peak in 2026
data.description: On April 16, Jin10 reported that Export Development Canada, a Canadian export credit agency, has released its spring outlook predicting that strong safe-haven demand will keep gold prices elevated this year. According to the agency, after reaching an average price of $3,435 per ounce in 2025, gold prices are expected to average around $4,990 per ounce in 2026. The agency anticipates that gold will peak in 2026, and as uncertainties ease and high prices suppress demand, prices are projected to gradually decline to an average of $4,689 per ounce in 2027. Over time, jewelry demand is expected to regain its traditional role as the main source of gold demand, while investment-driven volatility is likely to diminish.

data.title: ECB Officials: War Fundamentally Alters Inflation Outlook
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has released its meeting minutes, revealing that officials believe the ongoing war has fundamentally changed the inflation outlook. According to Jin10, the ECB emphasized that the conflict has introduced significant uncertainties and challenges to the economic landscape, impacting inflation expectations. The officials noted that the war's effects on supply chains and energy prices are contributing to the altered inflation dynamics. The ECB is closely monitoring these developments to assess their implications for monetary policy and economic stability.

data.title: UK Financial Conduct Authority Seeks Input on Crypto Asset Regulation
data.description:
The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has released a new consultation paper on the regulation of crypto assets. According to BlockBeats, the consultation seeks opinions on the scope of regulated digital asset activities within the regulatory roadmap framework. Key areas of focus include stablecoin issuance, trading platforms, custody, and staking activities. The consultation is open to individuals, businesses, industry groups, policymakers, and academics, with a deadline of June 3, 2026. Crypto firms can begin applying for FCA authorization from September 30, 2026.

This consultation marks the latest development in the UK's crypto regulatory roadmap. Earlier this year, the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Crypto Assets) Regulations 2026 came into effect, bringing new types of crypto asset activities under FCA oversight. The FCA has indicated that substantial work on the future crypto regulatory framework's rules is nearly complete, with related policy statements expected this summer and a final policy statement anticipated in the fall. The FCA emphasized that until the new regulatory framework is fully implemented, cryptocurrencies largely remain in a regulatory void, governed only by financial promotion and financial crime regulations.

Additionally, the FCA plans to conduct another round of consultations later this year on decentralized finance (DeFi), the operational resilience of distributed ledger technology enterprises, and updates to financial crime guidelines.

data.title: BTTC Bridge Enhances Transaction Tracking Features
data.description: BTTC Bridge has launched an upgraded transaction record page to improve asset tracking for cross-chain users. According to ChainCatcher, the update introduces dual-view modes, 'All Records' and 'In Progress,' along with multi-dimensional filtering tools. These enhancements allow users to search transactions by status, type, and custom date ranges. The page now also features distinct identifiers for deposits, withdrawals, and cross-chain activities, facilitating quicker fund tracking. Users can now access the platform for a clearer cross-chain transaction management experience. The team plans to continue refining product details to support broader on-chain interactions.
